spots.name: Sapporo Odori Park
spots.trivia: A 1.5 km green corridor through central Sapporo, famous for the Snow Festival ice sculptures every February.

spots.name: Hokkaido Shrine
spots.trivia: The most important shrine in Hokkaido, enshrining the development gods of the island. Set within a forested park.

spots.name: Goryokaku Fort
spots.trivia: Japan's first Western-style star fort, built in 1864 in a five-pointed star shape visible from the observation tower.

spots.name: Noboribetsu Hell Valley
spots.trivia: A volcanic valley with boiling sulphur pools and steaming vents. Oni demon statues guard the otherworldly landscape.

spots.name: Shiroi Koibito Park
spots.trivia: A fanciful European-castle themed confectionery park celebrating Hokkaido's most famous chocolate cookie.

spots.name: Nijo Fish Market
spots.trivia: Sapporo's oldest fish market selling the finest Hokkaido sea urchin, crab, and salmon directly from the boats.

spots.name: Shikisai-no-Oka Farm
spots.trivia: A flower farm in Biei with seasonal waves of lavender, sunflowers, and rainbow-coloured fields across rolling hills.

spots.name: Blue Pond Biei
spots.trivia: An otherworldly pond of stunning cobalt blue caused by aluminium hydroxide, surrounded by dead silver birch trunks.

spots.name: Daisetsuzan Grand Hotel
spots.trivia: Gateway to Daisetsuzan National Park, Japan's largest national park featuring active volcanoes and alpine meadows.

spots.name: Kushiro Wetlands
spots.trivia: Japan's largest wetland, a UNESCO Ramsar site and the habitat of the endangered red-crowned crane.

spots.name: Lake Toya
spots.trivia: A circular caldera lake around a volcanic island. Fireworks are launched over the lake every night during summer.

spots.name: Hakodate Morning Market
spots.trivia: Hakodate's beloved morning market with hundreds of fresh seafood stalls opening at 5am daily.

spots.name: Trappistine Convent
spots.trivia: A 19th-century French-style convent famous for its butter cookies and the tree-lined avenue leading to its gates.

spots.name: Hokkaido University Ginkgo
spots.trivia: A stunning avenue of golden ginkgo trees winding through the historic Hokkaido University campus in Sapporo.

spots.name: Sahoro Resort Bear Mountain
spots.trivia: A wildlife sanctuary where brown bears roam freely across vast open fields, observable from elevated catwalks.

spots.name: Otaru Canal
spots.trivia: A 19th-century canal lined with restored stone warehouses converted into romantic restaurants and glass shops.

spots.name: Yoichi Distillery
spots.trivia: Where Masataka Taketsuru founded Nikka Whisky in 1934 after studying Scottish distillation craft. Free tastings available.

spots.name: Hokkaido Shrine Sapporo
spots.trivia: Inner precinct shrine within the forested Maruyama Park, a tranquil escape in the heart of Sapporo.

spots.name: Rebun Island Kagome Park
spots.trivia: On Japan's northernmost island, alpine flowers bloom at sea level every June due to the unique Arctic climate.

spots.name: Abashiri Prison Museum
spots.trivia: The preserved buildings of Hokkaido's notorious 19th-century prison, where convicts built the island's roads.
